mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates rising labor income and aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are not positive in at least 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate persisting below 0.5 percent anchors the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing risk-free yields and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls signal synchronized demand and income expansion that lifts corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not positive in at least 60% of months when both RSAFS and PAYEMS increase month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, accelerating consumer price inflation reduces real corporate earnings and signals impending monetary tightening, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of late-cycle months with accelerating CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below its trailing 12-month average signals manufacturing contraction and negative operating leverage, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO declines below its 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate falling below 5.0 percent signals a tight labor market that generates wage-cost pressure on corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods when UNRATE is below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with a federal funds rate below 1 percent indicates monetary policy is behind the curve, prompting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as recession risk is repriced.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp over 2 months while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE rises while FEDFUNDS stays below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1200 thousand units signals accelerating construction activity and positive wealth effects that drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances when HOUST exceeds 1200.
